title: Tail Risk Analytics Module: Institutional-Grade Downside Risk Metrics
hypothesis: The Aegis engine computes only Sharpe, VaR/CVaR, and max drawdown — standard metrics that miss critical dimensions of tail risk used by institutional investors. Adding Sortino, Omega, Calmar ratios plus drawdown duration, tail concentration, and ulcer index will provide a significantly more complete risk picture.

what_was_built.new_service: backend/services/tail_risk.py — 14 tail risk metrics computed from daily returns
metrics_added: Sortino Ratio (risk-adjusted return using only downside deviation)
Calmar Ratio (annualized return / max drawdown, standard at hedge funds)
Downside Deviation (annualized volatility of negative returns only)
Maximum Drawdown Duration (longest recovery period in trading days)
Tail Concentration Index (% of losses in worst 5% of days — fat tail detector)
Ulcer Index (RMS of drawdown %, Peter Martin 1987)
Win Rate, Average Win/Loss, Profit Factor
integration_points: portfolio_engine.py analyze_portfolio() — returns tail_risk dict with all metrics
stock_analyzer.py analyze_stock() — returns tail_risk dict per ticker
what_was_built.tests: 29 new tests in test_tail_risk.py covering all metrics, edge cases, mathematical consistency

why_this_matters: Sharpe ratio treats upside and downside volatility equally — Sortino does not
Max drawdown alone misses HOW LONG recovery takes — drawdown duration captures this
Omega ratio captures the full return distribution, not just mean/variance
Tail concentration reveals whether risk is distributed (Gaussian) or concentrated (fat-tailed)
Calmar ratio is the standard hedge fund performance metric — was completely absent
Ulcer index penalizes both depth AND duration of drawdowns unlike simple max DD
what_it_does_NOT_do: No copula / cross-asset tail dependence (would need multivariate analysis)
No dynamic risk budgeting based on these metrics
No frontend visualization yet (ready for frontend integration)
No historical comparison of tail metrics across regimes
self_critique: The metrics are computed from historical daily returns — they are backward-looking by nature
Tail concentration uses a simple percentile cutoff rather than EVT (Extreme Value Theory)
Could add conditional tail metrics stratified by market regime (bull/bear/volatile)
Frontend needs charts to visualize these — raw numbers in JSON are useful but not user-friendly
next_steps: Frontend: add tail risk panel to stock detail and portfolio analysis pages
Regime-conditional tail metrics: compute Sortino/Omega separately for bull vs bear periods
EVT-based tail modeling (GPD fit to extreme losses) for more rigorous tail quantification
Cross-asset tail dependence using Student-t copulas for portfolio-level tail risk
Dynamic signal adjustment: use tail metrics to modulate signal weights (high Ulcer → more cautious)
references: Keating & Shadwick (2002) — "A Universal Performance Measure" (Omega ratio)
Sortino & Price (1994) — "Performance Measurement in a Downside Risk Framework"
Peter Martin (1987) — Ulcer Index, "The Investors Guide to Fidelity Funds"
